clinical_criteria: At least 2 physician-entered T2DM diagnoses required for some case paths
T2DM medication prescribed before T1DM medication (temporal requirement)
No T1DM diagnosis codes allowed for T2DM cases
algorithm_summary: The Northwestern University T2DM algorithm identifies Type 2 diabetes cases and controls from EMR data using a multi-path decision tree. For cases, it requires: (1) absence of T1DM diagnoses, (2) presence of T2DM diagnoses and/or medications, (3) abnormal lab values meeting diabetes thresholds, and (4) temporal ordering where T2DM medications precede T1DM medications when both are present. The algorithm has 5 distinct paths for case identification. For controls, it excludes patients with any diabetes diagnoses, diabetes medications, diabetes supplies, abnormal glucose/HbA1c labs, or diabetes family history, while requiring adequate clinical encounter history. Laboratory thresholds are stricter for controls (glucose >=110 mg/dl, HbA1c >=6.0%) compared to cases.
